metadata.title: States PCS: Himachal Pradesh GK for HPPSC Exams

questions.question: Why did people of Rampur Bushahr revolt against their rulers?
questions.explanation: People of Rampur Bushahr revolted against their rulers in 1859 opposing the Begar system in the state

questions.question: People of which of the following states revolted against the policies of Wazir Gulam Qadir?
questions.explanation: People of Hundur(Nalagarh) revolted in 1859 against the corrupt and despotic policies of Wazir Gulam Qadir

questions.question: When did Mandi agitation took place?
questions.explanation: Mandi agitation took place in 1909-1910. People of Mandi protested against Raja Bhawani Singh.

questions.question: Himachal Pradesh became the _____ state of Indian Union?
questions.explanation: Himachal Pradesh became the 18th state of Indian Union on 25th January 1971

questions.question: Who was the first governor of Himachal Pradesh?
questions.explanation: S Chakravarti became the the first governor of Himachal Pradesh after Himachal Pradesh attained full statehood.

questions.question: Nargu sanctuary is located in which district of Himachal Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Nargu sanctuary is located in Mandi district of Himachal Pradesh

questions.question: Which is the only sanctuary in Himachal Pradesh reporting the presence of Kashmir Stag?
questions.explanation: Gamgul Siahbehi Sanctuary in Chamba district is the only sanctuary in Himachal Pradesh reporting the presence of Kashmir Stag

questions.question: Which of the following is the smallest wildllife sanctuary in Himachal pradesh?
questions.explanation: Shilli wildlife sanctuary located in Solan district is the smallest wildllife sanctuary in Himachal pradesh

questions.question: Gasota fair is associated with which of the following places?
questions.explanation: Gasota fair is associated with Gasota in Hamirpur district and takes place in the month of May

questions.question: Bhunda festival of Nirmand takes place after how many years?
questions.explanation: Bhunda festival of Nirmand, an important festival of hill people, takes place at an interval of Twelve years.
